name: Angkor Wat
khmerName: អង្គរវត្ត
description: The largest religious monument in the world, originally built as a Hindu temple to Vishnu, later becoming a Buddhist site.

name: Phnom Bakheng
khmerName: ភ្នំបាខែង
description: Hilltop temple famous for sunset views over Angkor Wat, dedicated to Shiva.

name: Bayon
khmerName: ប្រាសាទបាយ័ន
description: Known for its 216 serene stone faces, located at the exact center of Angkor Thom.

name: Baphuon
khmerName: ប្រាសាទបាភូន
description: A three-tiered temple mountain with a giant reclining Buddha built into its western face.

name: Phimeanakas
khmerName: ប្រាសាទភិមានអាកាស
description: A Hindu temple in the pyramidal Khleang style, once part of the Royal Palace enclosure.

name: Terrace of the Elephants
khmerName: លានដំរី
description: A 350m long viewing platform decorated with carved elephants, used for public ceremonies.

name: Terrace of the Leper King
khmerName: លានស្តេចគំនួច
description: A platform with elaborate carvings, named after a statue found on the site.

name: Ta Keo
khmerName: ប្រាសាទតាកែវ
description: An unfinished temple mountain known for its massive, undecorated sandstone tiers.

name: Ta Prohm
khmerName: ប្រាសាទតាព្រហ្ម
description: Famous for massive strangler fig and silk-cotton tree roots overtaking the ruins, left largely as it was found.

name: Banteay Kdei
khmerName: ប្រាសាទបន្ទាយក្តី
description: A Buddhist monastic complex with concentric enclosure walls, quieter than nearby sites.

name: Srah Srang
khmerName: ស្រះស្រង់
description: A royal bathing pool with a sandstone landing platform, popular at sunrise.

name: Preah Khan
khmerName: ប្រាសាទព្រះខ័ន
description: A sprawling flat temple complex that served as a Buddhist university and city.

name: Neak Pean
khmerName: ប្រាសាទនាគព័ន្ធ
description: A small artificial island temple with a central pool encircled by two naga serpents.

name: Ta Som
khmerName: ប្រាសាទតាសោម
description: A small single-shrine temple, notable for a tree overtaking its eastern gate tower.

name: East Mebon
khmerName: ប្រាសាទមេបុណ្យខាងកើត
description: A temple mountain that once stood on an island in the now-dry Eastern Baray reservoir, guarded by stone elephants.

name: Pre Rup
khmerName: ប្រាសាទប្រែរូប
description: A temple mountain in pink sandstone and brick, a strong sunset viewpoint.

name: Banteay Srei
khmerName: ប្រាសាទបន្ទាយស្រី
description: A small temple famed for exceptionally intricate pink sandstone carvings, located further from the main complex.

name: Preah Ko
khmerName: ប្រាសាទព្រះគោ
description: The earliest temple of the Roluos Group, dedicated to ancestral kings, with six brick towers.

name: Bakong
khmerName: ប្រាសាទបាគង
description: The first temple mountain built by the Khmer, a model for later state temples.

name: Lolei
khmerName: ប្រាសាទលលៃ
description: Brick tower temple originally on an island in a large baray, now on RUPP's outer route.
